title: viguiera cordata var. websteri
intro: Viguiera cordata var. websteri, also known as the Webster's Viguiera, is a variety of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is known to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving pain, and has been used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Viguiera cordata var. websteri is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
